title: Implement M1 save replay and Worker headless parity harness
summary: Wire the M1 kernel through save/load, replay checkpoints, Worker execution and Node headless execution so the hauling/building scenario has identical authoritative hash.
acceptance: Node headless and browser Worker run the same hauling/building command stream to the same periodic world hash
Save at an interruption point, load, rebuild indexes and resume produces the same final hash as uninterrupted replay
Snapshot and UI detail remain read-only projections and cannot mutate authoritative state
Diagnostics output seed, scenario id, first divergent tick and artifact paths on failure

history.details: Implemented focused M1 save/replay and Worker/headless parity harness; required checks pass: typecheck, m1-save-replay, worker-smoke, sim:replay-test, taskctl validate, git diff --check; extra gates format:check and lint pass.

changes_requested: High: loadM1HaulingBuildingSaveEnvelope accepts tampered nested read-only projection payloads while returning ok; Medium: worker-smoke M1 parity uses in-process worker only and does not instantiate browser-worker-entry in a real browser Worker.

Fixed reviewer findings: nested read-only projection tampering now fails closed, and worker-smoke now covers a real Chromium module Worker using browser-worker-entry.ts; required checks pass including typecheck, m1-save-replay, worker-smoke, sim:replay-test, taskctl validate and git diff --check; format:check and lint also pass.
verified: No blocking findings on re-review; nested save projection validation now fails closed, real Chromium module Worker parity is covered, and required checks plus format/lint pass.

review.summary: No blocking findings on re-review; nested save projection validation now fails closed, real Chromium module Worker parity is covered, and required checks plus format/lint pass.
